rag_rules: Resolve all references to previous values using pre() or similar temporal operators.
Ensure type consistency in binary expressions involving temporal or arithmetic operations.
Validate that all identifiers used in AGREE contracts are properly declared and visible in scope.
generic_rules: Use pre() to reference previous values in temporal contracts.
Align expression types with expected types in AGREE assumptions and guarantees.
Verify all identifiers are declared before use in AGREE annexes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Binary expressions in AGREE must have operands of matching types.
AGREE contracts must reference valid, visible identifiers in their scope.
Temporal references like pre() must be used for previous state values.
anti_patterns: Avoid using undeclared or improperly scoped identifiers in AGREE contracts.
Do not mix incompatible types (e.g., real and int) in binary expressions without explicit conversion.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
